message:
fix(tmux): use declared GT_PANE_ID for agent liveness instead of process-tree inference (gt-qmsx)

ZFC violation: IsRuntimeRunning and FindAgentPane scanned all tmux panes
and crawled process trees to infer which pane ran the agent. This is
cognition in Go - the agent identity should be declared, not guessed.

Changes:
- Record GT_PANE_ID in tmux session env at startup across all session
  creation paths (polecat, deacon, daemon, crew, boot via StartSession)
- IsRuntimeRunning: check declared pane first, fall back to scan for
  legacy sessions without GT_PANE_ID
- FindAgentPane: read declared pane_id first, fall back to scan
- Extract findAgentPaneByScan and checkTargetPaneForRuntime helpers
- Add GetPaneID to deacon tmuxOps interface and mock

Legacy sessions (started before this change) continue to work via the
fallback scan path. Process tree crawling within a single pane is
unchanged (downstream bead gt-sk5u).

files.patch:
@@ -2007,6 +2007,11 @@ func (d *Daemon) restartPolecatSession(rigName, polecatName, sessionName string)
 	processNames := config.ResolveProcessNames(rc.ResolvedAgent, rc.Command)
 	_ = d.tmux.SetEnvironment(sessionName, "GT_PROCESS_NAMES", strings.Join(processNames, ","))
 
+	// Record agent's pane_id for ZFC-compliant liveness checks (gt-qmsx).
+	if paneID, err := d.tmux.GetPaneID(sessionName); err == nil {
+		_ = d.tmux.SetEnvironment(sessionName, "GT_PANE_ID", paneID)
+	}
+
 	// Apply theme
 	theme := tmux.AssignTheme(rigName)
 	_ = d.tmux.ConfigureGasTownSession(sessionName, theme, rigName, polecatName, "polecat")
